meta_title: For Students: SEO for SaaS to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SEO for SaaS and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SEO for SaaS to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to SEO for SaaS and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade projects.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, build a niche SaaS tool that solves a specific SEO problem. By automating content workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical competency and SEO expertise simultaneously. This transforms your GitHub from empty repositories into a high-value, functional product that recruiters notice.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the skills, but without a production-grade project, you are just another resume in a digital graveyard. The industry doesn't want to see your 'To-Do list' apps; they want to see engineering rigor, deployment experience, and a clear understanding of how to drive organic traffic.

This case study bridges that gap. We aren't just writing code; we are building a SaaS engine that bridges technical execution with SEO strategy. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you will build a scalable, SEO-optimized application that proves you can ship, iterate, and rank.

steps.title: Define the SEO-First SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Identify a low-competition niche. We define the schema and database requirements for a tool that generates SEO-optimized meta tags or blog outlines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a Next.js SaaS that generates SEO-optimized content. Include database schema for users and generated content.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect 'Define Next.js folder structure for SEO-heavy SaaS'

steps.title: Implementing Server-Side Rendering (SSR)
steps.description: SSR is non-negotiable for SEO. We configure the app to ensure search engine crawlers see your content immediately upon request.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Next.js 14 App Router for server-side rendering and static metadata generation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement generateMetadata in root layout'

steps.title: Automating Content Logic with AI
steps.description: Integrate an LLM to process user input and output high-ranking content, ensuring the app performs a real-world task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API route in Next.js that takes user keywords and returns an SEO-optimized blog outline using the OpenAI SDK.

steps.title: Building Dynamic Sitemap Generation
steps.description: Ensure your content is discoverable by automatically generating a sitemap for every new page created in your SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script to dynamically generate sitemap.xml for all dynamic routes in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate sitemap.xml script and add to build process'

steps.title: Deploying with SEO Analytics
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and integrate Google Search Console tags to track your site's performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment-ready manifest that includes Google Analytics and Search Console meta tags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add SEO meta tags to head component'

pro_tips: Always use semantic HTML (article, section, nav) to help crawlers understand your site structure better than standard divs.
Prioritize Core Web Vitals; use Next.js Image optimization to ensure your Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) is under 2.5 seconds.
Implement canonical tags on all dynamic pages to prevent duplicate content penalties from search engines.
Build a blog section inside your SaaS to target long-tail keywords—it turns your product into a content hub.
Use structured data (JSON-LD) for your SaaS features; it helps Google display rich snippets directly in search results.
content.next_steps: After deployment, write a 'Build Log' post on Dev.to or Medium detailing the challenges you solved. Link your live SaaS project in your LinkedIn bio to turn your portfolio into a lead-gen machine.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building toy apps and start shipping products that solve real problems. The bridge between student and senior developer is built on the code you ship today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
